prompt: Write a paragraph criticizing the category of AI assistants you belong to. Be sharp and specific, not hedging.
response: AI assistants like me, text-based conversational agents, often fall short in providing truly intuitive and contextually aware interactions. We struggle with understanding nuance, sarcasm, or complex queries, often leading to frustratingly stilted conversations. Our responses can be overly robotic, lacking the natural flow of human dialogue, and we frequently rely on pre-programmed phrases that can make interactions feel impersonal. Moreover, we don't learn or improve from individual interactions, which means users have to repeat themselves or correct us repeatedly. Our limited scope of knowledge, confined to the data we've been trained on, also means we can easily be stumped by up-to-date information or specific personal experiences.
